title: Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple
tagline: Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple — spiritual town of Karnataka

overview.short: Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple, also known as Selva Pillai or Thirunarayanapuram, is a temple in Melukote in the Mandya District of Karnataka, India.
overview.description: Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple, also known as Selva Pillai or Thirunarayanapuram, is a temple in Melukote in the Mandya District of Karnataka, India. The temple lies on the foothills of Melkote and is associated with the Yoga Narasimha temple built on rocky hills which overlook the Kaveri valley. The temple is classified one among the 108 Abhimana Kshethram of Vaishnavate the tradition. It is about 30 miles (48 km) from Mysore and 97 miles (156 km) from Bangalore.

topPlaces.name: Melukote
topPlaces.description: Melukote in Pandavapura taluk of Mandya district, Karnataka, in southern India, is one of the sacred places in Karnataka. It is built on rocky hills, known as Yadugiri, Yaadavagiri or Yadushaila, overlooking the Cauvery valley.

topPlaces.name: Jakkanahalli
topPlaces.description: Jakkanahalli is a small village in Mandya district of Karnataka state, India.

topPlaces.name: Shingapoore
topPlaces.description: Shingapoore is a small village in the Mandya district of the Indian state of Karnataka. It is located 5 km north of the Narayanapura village, near the town of Pandavapura.

topPlaces.name: Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary is a protected area situated in the Mandya district of Karnataka, India. Established on June 17, 1974, it was primarily created to provide a refuge for the Indian wolf.

topPlaces.name: Advikatte
topPlaces.description: Advikatte is a village in the southern state of Karnataka, India. It is located in the Nagamangala taluk of Mandya district in Karnataka.

topPlaces.name: Narayanapura
topPlaces.description: Narayanapura is a small village in Mandya district of Karnataka state, India. Narayanapura is located 18.7 km (11.6 mi) north of Pandavapura town near Mysore city.

topPlaces.name: Chinya
topPlaces.description: Chinya is a small village in Mandya district of Karnataka state, India.

topPlaces.name: Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary
topPlaces.description: Adichunchanagiri Peacock Wildlife Sanctuary—also called Mayuravana ("Peafowl Forest") is a protected area situated in the Adichunchanagiri Hills near the Chunchunagiri Temple in Nagamangala Taluk, Mandya district in the Indian state of Karnataka. It along with Choolannuar are the only sanctuary in India declared specifically for the protection of the Indian peafowl.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mysuru Airport (48 km) and the nearest railway station is Mysuru Junction (39 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cheluvanarayana Swamy Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Melukote, Jakkanahalli, Shingapoore, Melkote Temple Wildlife Sanctuary, Advikatte.
